feed.title: Google Cloud: Cloud Database Migration Service
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Cloud Database Migration Service

entry.content: You can use the Database Migration Service MCP server to enable agents and AI applications to view and manage running migration jobs. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: The Database Migration Service MCP server is now available in Preview, allowing agents and AI applications to view and manage migration jobs. This feature enhances the control and automation capabilities for your database migration processes.
entry.title: Feature: Manage database migrations with MCP server

entry.content: Database Migration Service for homogeneous MySQL migrations now lets you migrate individual databases from your source. You can select the databases when you create a migration job for homogeneous MySQL migrations.
entry.summary: Database Migration Service now supports migrating individual databases for homogeneous MySQL migrations. You can select specific databases when creating a new migration job, offering more flexibility in your migration process.
entry.title: Feature: Migrate Individual MySQL Databases

entry.content:
Database Migration Service for heterogeneous SQL Server migrations now supports failback migration jobs in Preview. Failback migrations let you push CDC updates back to the original SQL Server source from the destination PostgreSQL database after you complete the standard migration. This feature keeps your original source database alive and up to date in case you need to switch your application back to the source SQL Server database. For more information, see the page relevant for your migration scenario:

  • Failback migration guide for SQL Server to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL
  • Failback migration guide for SQL Server to AlloyDB for PostgreSQL
entry.summary: Database Migration Service now supports failback for SQL Server migrations, allowing you to push CDC updates back to your original SQL Server source from PostgreSQL. This feature keeps your source database up-to-date, enabling a potential switch back if needed. It's currently available in preview for migrations to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL and AlloyDB for PostgreSQL.
entry.title: Announcement: SQL Server Failback Migration Now in Preview

entry.content:
Database Migration Service for heterogeneous migrations now supports RENAME-, MOVE-, CASE_HANDLING and GENERATE_MISSING_PK directives to provide improved control over converting Oracle and SQL Server objects to PostgreSQL. For more information, see the page relevant for your migration scenario:

  • Supported conversion directives for Oracle to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL
  • Supported conversion directives for Oracle to AlloyDB for PostgreSQL
  • Supported conversion directives for SQL Server to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL
  • Supported conversion directives for SQL Server to AlloyDB for PostgreSQL
entry.summary: Database Migration Service now offers new directives for heterogeneous migrations, including RENAME-, MOVE-, CASE_HANDLING, and GENERATE_MISSING_PK. These enhancements provide greater control when converting Oracle and SQL Server objects to PostgreSQL. Learn more about how these directives can improve your migration process.
entry.title: Feature: New directives for database migrations

entry.content:
Database Migration Service now supports the one-time migration type for all heterogeneous migration scenarios. For more information, see the following pages:

  • Migration data flow for Oracle to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL
  • Migration data flow for Oracle to AlloyDB for PostgreSQL
  • Migration data flow for SQL Server to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL
  • Migration data flow for SQL Server to AlloyDB for PostgreSQL
entry.summary: Database Migration Service has expanded its capabilities to include one-time migrations, a feature now available for all heterogeneous migration scenarios. This enhancement simplifies the process of moving databases between different systems.
entry.title: Feature: Database Migration Service now supports one-time migrations for all heterogeneous scenarios

entry.content: Database Migration Service for homogeneous PostgreSQL migrations to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL now supports PostgreSQL version 18. For more information, see Supported source and destination databases.
entry.summary: Database Migration Service now supports PostgreSQL version 18 for homogeneous migrations to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL.
entry.title: Feature: PostgreSQL 18 Support

entry.content: Database Migration Service for homogeneous MySQL migrations to Cloud SQL for MySQL now supports MySQL version 8.0.43. For more information, see Supported source and destination databases.
entry.summary: The Database Migration Service has been updated to support homogeneous MySQL migrations to Cloud SQL for MySQL version 8.0.43.
entry.title: Feature: Database Migration Service now supports MySQL 8.0.43

entry.content:
Database Migration Service now supports Private Service Connect interfaces for source network connectivity in all heterogeneous migration scenarios.

For more information, see the following pages:

  • Source connectivity methods for Oracle to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL
  • Source connectivity methods for Oracle to AlloyDB for PostgreSQL
  • Source connectivity methods for SQL Server to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL
  • Source connectivity methods for SQL Server to AlloyDB for PostgreSQL
entry.summary: Database Migration Service now supports Private Service Connect interfaces for source network connectivity in all heterogeneous migration scenarios.
entry.title: Feature: Database Migration Service now supports Private Service Connect

entry.content:
Gemini-powered conversion features for heterogeneous migrations in Database Migration Service are now generally available (GA).

For more information, see Accelerate code and schema conversion with Gemini.
entry.summary: Gemini-powered conversion features for heterogeneous migrations in Database Migration Service are now generally available.
entry.title: Announcement: Gemini-powered conversion features for heterogeneous migrations

entry.content:
Database Migration Service for heterogeneous Oracle and SQL Server migrations now supports table-level observability features. In addition to metrics aggregated for the whole database, you can now monitor the migration progress individually for each table. For more information, see the monitoring pages for your scenario:

  • Migration job metrics for Oracle to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL
  • Migration job metrics for Oracle to AlloyDB for PostgreSQL
  • Migration job metrics for SQL Server to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL
  • Migration job metrics for SQL Server to AlloyDB for PostgreSQL
entry.summary: You can now monitor the migration progress of individual tables during heterogeneous Oracle and SQL Server migrations. This provides more granular visibility beyond overall database metrics.
entry.title: Feature: Table-level observability for heterogeneous database migrations

entry.content: Database Migration Service for homogeneous PostgreSQL migrations to AlloyDB for PostgreSQL now supports PostgreSQL version 17. For more information, see Supported source and destination databases.
entry.summary: Database migration for PostgreSQL to AlloyDB now supports version 17.
entry.title: Feature: New PostgreSQL support

entry.content:
Database Migration Service now supports Private Service Connect interfaces for network connectivity in homogeneous Cloud SQL for MySQL, Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL, and AlloyDB for PostgreSQL migrations. For more information, see the following pages:

  • Networking methods in homogeneous Cloud SQL for MySQL migrations
  • Networking methods in homogeneous Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL migrations
  • Networking methods in homogeneous AlloyDB for PostgreSQL migrations
entry.summary: Database Migration Service now supports Private Service Connect interfaces for network connectivity.
entry.title: Feature: New Private Service Connect Support

entry.content:
Database Migration Service support for heterogeneous SQL Server to PostgreSQL migrations is now generally available (GA).

For more information, see:

  • Database Migration Service for SQL Server to Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL
  • Database Migration Service for SQL Server to AlloyDB for PostgreSQL
entry.summary: Database Migration Service now supports heterogeneous SQL Server to PostgreSQL migrations, including to Cloud SQL and AlloyDB for PostgreSQL. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Announcement: Database Migration Service now supports SQL Server to PostgreSQL

entry.content: Heterogeneous Oracle and SQL Server migrations now provide an improved experience with conversion overview dashboards and streamlined conversion issue grouping. To learn more about Database Migration Service conversion workspaces with Gemini-powered conversion features, see Convert SQL with Database Migration Service .
entry.summary: Database Migration Service now offers an enhanced experience for migrating Oracle and SQL Server databases, featuring new conversion overview dashboards and better issue grouping.
entry.title: Feature: Improved Database Migration Experience
